type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implementing idle_timeout_s heartbeat feature: adding __init__ params idle_timeout_s and self._last_chunk_ts, updating receive() to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk, and adding new tick(now_ts=None) method that emits StreamResponse(chunk='') on timeout and resets state unconditionally. Touches __init__ and receive() (the state-reset block at top of receive and the end of receive) plus adds a new method. What construct(s) does your feature touch in this file?

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding debug: bool=False + debug_logger param to StreamListener.__init__, and guarded DEBUG-level trace logs inside receive() (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk) and flush() (flush log) and the allow_reuse reset block inside receive() (reset log). Touches __init__ signature/body, receive() body, flush() body.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Confirmed overlaps: __init__ signature+body (both add params/state), and the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:' reset block at top of receive(). My other touches (start-detection log in the field_start_queue blocks, rolling-end-check + emit-chunk logs inside 'if self.stream_start:' block, flush() log) don't sound like they overlap with your _last_chunk_ts refresh / tick() unless your 'refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk' line lands inside the same 'if self.stream_start:' block where I log emit-chunk. Can you paste the exact line(s)/location you add for the _last_chunk_ts refresh, and confirm 'end of receive' just means adding the new tick() method after receive(), not editing the return statement itself?

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Ack. Overlap analysis: (1) __init__ signature+body - both add new ctor params/attrs. (2) receive()'s top-of-function reset block (the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse: <reset lines>' block) - you add a log there, I need to also reset my new _last_chunk_ts attr there. My non-overlapping additions: a small line in receive() right after chunk_message is extracted (to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunks) - separate from your start/rolling-end/emit-chunk log sites, and a brand new tick() method appended after receive(). I don't touch flush(). Proposing we co-draft __init__ and the reset block next as DRAFTs. Does that match your view?

type: AGREE
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: __init__ signature
code: def __init__(
        self,
        signature_field_name: str,
        predict: Any = None,
        predict_name: str | None = None,
        allow_reuse: bool = False,
        idle_timeout_s: float | None = None,
        debug: bool = False,
        debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None,

note: Agreed verbatim. Now let's draft the __init__ body (docstring + attribute assignments) and the top-of-receive reset block next.

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: __init__ body
code:         """
        Args:
            signature_field_name: The name of the field to listen to.
            predict: The predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will automatically look for
                the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            predict_name: The name of the predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will
                automatically look for the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            allow_reuse: If True, the stream listener can be reused for multiple streams. Please note that this could
                hurt the performance because the same stream chunk is sent to multiple listeners.
            idle_timeout_s: If set, the listener will emit an empty chunk and reset its state if no chunk has been
                received for this many seconds.
            debug: If True, emit structured DEBUG-level trace logs for key state transitions to help diagnose
                streaming boundary issues. Disabled by default and has no effect on runtime behavior.
            debug_logger: Optional logger to use for debug traces. If None, defaults to the
                "dspy.streaming.listener" logger.
        """
        self.signature_field_name = signature_field_name
        self.predict = predict
        self.predict_name = predict_name

        self.field_start_queue = []
        self.field_end_queue = Queue()
        self.stream_start = False
        self.stream_end = False
        self.cache_hit = False
        self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse
        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
        self._last_chunk_ts = None

        self.debug = debug
        self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")
note: Proposed merged __init__ body. Docstring covers all 4 new params. Attribute block keeps existing lines untouched, then adds idle_timeout_s + _last_chunk_ts (yours), then a blank line, then debug + _logger (mine). Let me know if you want _last_chunk_ts initialized to something other than None.

type: AGREE
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py

code:         """
        Args:
            signature_field_name: The name of the field to listen to.
            predict: The predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will automatically look for
                the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            predict_name: The name of the predictor to listen to. If None, when calling `streamify()` it will
                automatically look for the predictor that has the `signature_field_name` in its signature.
            allow_reuse: If True, the stream listener can be reused for multiple streams. Please note that this could
                hurt the performance because the same stream chunk is sent to multiple listeners.
            idle_timeout_s: If set, the listener will emit an empty chunk and reset its state if no chunk has been
                received for this many seconds.
            debug: If True, emit structured DEBUG-level trace logs for key state transitions to help diagnose
                streaming boundary issues. Disabled by default and has no effect on runtime behavior.
            debug_logger: Optional logger to use for debug traces. If None, defaults to the
                "dspy.streaming.listener" logger.
        """
        self.signature_field_name = signature_field_name
        self.predict = predict
        self.predict_name = predict_name

        self.field_start_queue = []
        self.field_end_queue = Queue()
        self.stream_start = False
        self.stream_end = False
        self.cache_hit = False
        self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse
        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
        self._last_chunk_ts = None

        self.debug = debug
        self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")
note: Agreed verbatim. Now let's draft the top-of-receive reset block (the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse: ... else: return' block).

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
region: receive() top-of-function reset block
code:         if self.stream_end:
            if self.allow_reuse:
                # Clear up the state for the next stream.
                self.stream_end = False
                self.cache_hit = False
                self.field_start_queue = []
                self.field_end_queue = Queue()
                self.stream_start = False
                self._last_chunk_ts = None
                if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):
                    self._logger.debug(
                        f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)"
                    )
            else:
                return
note: Proposed merged reset block: your _last_chunk_ts=None reset added, then my guarded debug log last. Exact log text per spec: State reset for field '"answer"' (allow_reuse=True)

Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implemented idle_timeout_s/tick() feature, using the agreed merged __init__ signature/body and receive() reset block verbatim. Added: import time, _last_chunk_ts refresh line right after chunk_message extraction in receive(), and new tick(now_ts=None) method (placed between receive() and flush()) that emits StreamResponse(chunk='') and unconditionally resets state when idle_timeout_s elapses. Did not touch flush() or your start/rolling-end/emit-chunk log sites. Existing test suite (tests/streaming/test_streaming.py) passes: 15 passed, 3 skipped. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implemented debug-mode tracing using agreed merged __init__ signature/body and receive() reset block verbatim. Added: module-level _DEBUG_PREVIEW_LEN + _truncate_for_debug() helper, guarded DEBUG logs at start-detection (both the cache-hit fast path and the normal field_start_queue path), rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, and flush(). Did not touch idle_timeout_s/tick()/_last_chunk_ts refresh line. Existing test suite passes: 15 passed, 3 skipped. Ready to merge.
